coredata.dc:title: Energy transfer mechanism of carboxymethyl chitosan-Eu3+/Tb3+ complex materials and application in multicolor LED
coredata.dc:creator: Wang, Jiaqi
Li, Yuanhang
Li, Xiaotong
Pan, Jiangbo
Wang, Di
Wei, Shuangying
Wang, Chengyu
Li, Jian
coredata.dc:description: Biological macromolecules had been studied as ligands in recent years, which not only give the complexes excellent polymer properties, but also have many advantages such as biodegradability. Carboxymethyl chitosan (CMCh) is excellent biological macromolecular ligand because of its abundant active amino and carboxyl groups, and it can smoothly transfer energy to Ln3+ after coordinating. To further study the energy transfer mechanism of CMCh-Ln3+ complexes, CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ complexes with different Eu3+/Tb3+ ratios were prepared by using CMCh as a ligand. The morphology, structure, and properties of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ were characterized and analyzed by infrared spectroscopy, XPS, TG and Judd-Ofelt theory, thus the chemical structure of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ was determined. The mechanism of energy transfer was explained in detail, also the Förster resonance transfer model is confirmed, and the hypothesis of energy transfer back was verified by the characterization and calculation methods of fluorescence spectra, UV spectra, phosphorescence spectra and fluorescence lifetime. Finally, CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ with different molar ratios were used to prepare a series of multicolor LED lamps, and it extends the application range of biological macromolecules as ligands.

head.ce:title: Energy transfer mechanism of carboxymethyl chitosan-Eu3+/Tb3+ complex materials and application in multicolor LED

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Biological macromolecules had been studied as ligands in recent years, which not only give the complexes excellent polymer properties, but also have many advantages such as biodegradability. Carboxymethyl chitosan (CMCh) is excellent biological macromolecular ligand because of its abundant active amino and carboxyl groups, and it can smoothly transfer energy to Ln3+ after coordinating. To further study the energy transfer mechanism of CMCh-Ln3+ complexes, CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ complexes with different Eu3+/Tb3+ ratios were prepared by using CMCh as a ligand. The morphology, structure, and properties of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ were characterized and analyzed by infrared spectroscopy, XPS, TG and Judd-Ofelt theory, thus the chemical structure of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ was determined. The mechanism of energy transfer was explained in detail, also the Förster resonance transfer model is confirmed, and the hypothesis of energy transfer back was verified by the characterization and calculation methods of fluorescence spectra, UV spectra, phosphorescence spectra and fluorescence lifetime. Finally, CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ with different molar ratios were used to prepare a series of multicolor LED lamps, and it extends the application range of biological macromolecules as ligands.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: Owing to the serious pollution of the global environment and the depletion of resources, the utilization of renewable resources has become one of the most important problems faced by human beings. Some natural polymers including cellulose, hemicellulose, lignin and chitosan and so on, have been widely used to manufacture new functional polymer materials (Yelatontsev, 2023). The monomers of renewable biomass or modified can be used as ligands to form complexes with various metal ions that is readily chelated. Biomacromolecule-metal complexes formed by biological macromolecule ligands and metal ions not only have good field luminescence characteristics of small molecule metal ion complexes, but also have excellent properties of polymeric materials, such as swelling property, viscoelasticity and so on. It can be used in catalysis (Wu et al., 2022; Zhang et al., 2022), electrochemistry (Wannasen, Swatsitang, & Pinitsoontorn, 2021), antibacterial (Korica et al., 2022; Pan, Xie, & Cai, 2022), adsorbing material (Duan et al., 2018; Naeimi & Amini, 2018), magnetic materials (Breijaert et al., 2022; Xu et al., 2022), luminescent materials (Rahmawati, Shih, & Imae, 2020) and other fields. Zhang et al. (2022). used ethyl cellulose as auxiliary ligands and substrate to sensitize the luminescence of Eu3+ and prepared a photoconversion film, which can convert harmful ultraviolet light into red light conducive to photosynthesis. The film is sensitive to pH and has a stable effect, which has a certain positive effect on agricultural production.

In these natural polymers, chitosan is the second most abundant natural biomass polymer on earth, which is obtained by deacetylation of chitin (Negm, Hefni, Abd-Elaal, Badr, & Abou Kana, 2020). It is eco-friendly, biodegradable, and easily modified owing to its structure contains a large number of amino (−NH2) and hydroxyl (-OH) groups. Carboxymethyl chitosan (CMCh) is one of the important chitosan derivatives, which is prepared by the reaction of chitosan and chloroacetic acid under alkaline conditions (Lee et al., 2023; Sahdev et al., 2022). CMCh possesses rich carboxyl groups so that the chemical reactivity of chitosan is improved and the coordination ability is enhanced on the basis of retaining the advantages of original chitosan, which has a strong affinity for most metal ions (Chen et al., 2022; Huang et al., 2023). Zhang et al. (2021) designed a self-assembled xerogel of H6L/Tb3+/CMCS (H6L = 5,5′,5″-(1,3,5-triazine-2,4,6-triyl)tris(azanediyl)triisophthalate), which was used as a sensor array to recognize 11 kinds of metal ions owing to the discriminant response of H6L/Tb3+/CMCS complex to different metal ions, and the addition of ligands significantly enhanced the fluorescence performance of Tb3+. This study showed that CMCS as a coligand produced hydrogen bond and electrostatic interaction with H6L and played a stabilizing role in H6L/Tb3+ complex but did not explicitly explain the process and mechanism of energy transfer between ligands and Tb3+. In 2021, Lai et al. (Lai, Ye, & Xiong, 2021) prepared fluorescent nanocomplexes of carboxymethyl cellulose (CMC)/Tb-Eu successfully. The “antenna effect” of CMC on Eu3+ and Tb3+ and the energy transfer from Tb3+ to Eu3+ were verified by fluorescence spectra and fluorescence lifetime, but the question of energy transfer back to the ligands was not mentioned.

CMCh-Eu3+ fluorescent aerogel was prepared in our previous research, and the mechanism of the effect of HCl and HAc on the fluorescence properties of the sample was elaborated, so as to realize the effective adsorption and identification of them (Li et al., 2021). It is worth mentioning that, the CMCh that has transferred energy to Ln3+ should not emit light in principle like CMC in literature (Lai et al., 2021), but the characteristic peak of CMCh at 436.5 nm appears in the fluorescence spectra, even the CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ with a certain Eu3+/Tb3+ ratio emits approximately white light under the UV light. It was hypothesized by us that the appearance of blue light emitting by the ligands is caused by the energy transfer back from the Ln3+ to the CMCh due to the lack of energy difference.

Since the energy difference between CMCh and Tb3+ is smaller than that between it and Eu3+, to verify the hypothesis mentioned above, Tb3+ with green light was introduced and CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ complexes with different Eu3+/Tb3+ ratios were prepared, exploring the coordination relationship, energy transfer mechanism between CMCh-Ln3+ and between Eu3+-Tb3+, also attempts have been made in the application of multicolor LED.

The CMCh (substitution degree =0.977 (Li, Zhang, et al., 2021), degree of deacetylation ≥90 %) was produced by Macklin (Shanghai, China). Eu(NO3)3·6H2O (99.99 %) was acquired from Huawei Ruike Chemical Co., Ltd. (Beijing, China). Tb(NO3)3·5H2O (99.99 %) was obtained from Baima Technology Co., Ltd. (Tianjin, China). Sulfuric acid gadolinium (Gd2(SO4)3; 99.9 %) and nitric acid (HNO3; AR) were purchased from Aladdin (Shanghai, China). The sodium hydroxide (NaOH; AR) was supplied by Tianli Chemical Reagents Co., Ltd. (Tianjin, China). Sulfuric acid (H2SO4; AR) was provided by Chemical Factory (Beijing, China). Epoxy resin AB adhesive was obtained from Lizhu flagship store on Alibaba. Ultrapure water supplied by Clever-Q30 UT was used in this study. All chemicals and solvents were used directly without purifying.

The molar ratio of Eu3+/Tb3+ (Eu3+: Tb3+ = 1: 0, 9: 1, 7: 3, 5: 5, 3: 7, 1: 9, 0: 1) was changed with the total molar ratio of the CMCh to Ln3+ fixed at 3: 1. The mixture was washed for three times and stirred for 1 h at room temperature. The solution was reacted at 70 °C for 1 h and was frozen for 24 h. Finally, the frozen samples were placed in the lyophilizer to obtain the powders. Then the molar of Tb3+ was changed to verify the sensitization of Tb3+ to Eu3+.

The pH was changed from 2 to 11 by using the 0.1 mol/L HNO3 and 0.1 mol/L NaOH, fixed the molar ratio of Eu3+/Tb3+ at 5: 5. Other conditions and experimental procedures were as described above. In this way, the effect of pH change on the properties of the CMCh-Eu3+/Td3+ was explored.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of multicolor LED lamps based on CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+
ce:section.ce:para: Using violet light chip (365 nm) as excitation source and CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ as color conversion layer, an adjustable multi-colors LED lamps were prepared successfully. Epoxy resin AB adhesive was mixed at equal mass of 1: 1 and CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ was dispersed in the epoxy resin with the weight ratio of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ to mixed epoxy resin AB adhesive at 1: 1. The mixture was coated on a violet light chip, and the multicolor LED lamps was obtained under drying at 50 °C for 12 h.

PerkinElmer Frontier spectrometer (PerkinElmer) was used to express the chemical bond properties of the sample. X-ray photoelectron spectrometer (XPS, Thermo Fisher Scientific, America) was used to characterize the chemical structure and coordination situation. LS-55 instrument (PerkinElmer) was employed to detect the fluorescence emission spectra of the CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+, with an excitation wavelength of 330 nm, slit width of 12.0 nm and 6.0 nm and scan speed of 1500 nm/min. Dual beam UV–visible spectrophotometer (TU-1901) was used to test the diffuse reflection of the sample, and the visible absorption spectra was transformed from diffuse reflection through the formula of Abs = (1-R)2/2R. A steady-state transient fluorescence spectrometer (FLS920, Edinburgh) was used for recording the luminescence lifetime. Phosphorescence spectra (77 K) of CMCh-Gd3+ was tested from steady-state transient fluorescence spectrophotometer (FLS920) with the excitation wavelength of 330 nm in an atmosphere of liquid nitrogen. CIE coordinates, emission spectra and chromaticity of LED lamp was recorded by spectral scintillation illuminometer (OHSP-350) at 3.4 V, 20 mA forward current.

In order to understand the variation of the functional groups of the sample, the infrared spectrogram of CMCh and CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ described that a wide absorption band at 3347 cm−1 moved to lower number at 3285 cm−1 after coordinating with Eu3+/Tb3+, corresponding stretching-vibration of hydroxyl OH and amino NH as shown in Fig. 1(a) (Sun & Wang, 2006). The peaks of anti-symmetric stretching υas(COO−) at 1593 cm−1 and symmetrical stretching υs(COO−) at 1419 cm−1 of carboxylate are moved to 1582 cm−1 and 1409 cm−1 respectively so as to form a more stable coordination structure (Mansur et al., 2017; Medeiros Borsagli, Mansur, Chagas, Oliveira, & Mansur, 2015). This indicates that when the carboxylic acid groups in the ligands perform coordination with Ln3+, part of the charge on CMCh flows to Ln3+ through the CO double bond, thus averaging its electron cloud density. When the CO double bond is weakened, it affects the conjugate structure of the entire ligand, causing the characteristic absorption peak to bathochromic shift. The peak at 1310 cm−1 belongs to CN asymmetric vibration of amino and amide bonds, which moves to 1321 cm−1 (Luo, Fan, Xiao, Sun, & Zhou, 2019). At the same time, the change of the peak from 1060 cm−1 to 1056 cm−1 is attributed to the movement of CO stretching vibration of the polysaccharide skeleton after coordinating with Eu3+/Tb3+ (Mansur, Ramanery, Oliveira, & Mansur, 2016). The above changes in characteristic peak position indicate that hydroxyl, amino, carboxyl and ether bonds on CMCh are involved in coordination to Ln3+. Owing to that vas(COO−)-vs(COO−) = 173 cm−1of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ is <200 cm−1 and equals to the △vas-s, the connection type between Eu3+/Tb3+ and carboxylate groups on CMCh is bidentate bridge connection (Li et al., 2013). To be more precise, the two oxygen atoms of the carboxylic acid groups are combined with the surface of the Eu3+/Tb3+ symmetrically and covalently (Basuny, Ali, Abd El-Gawad, Bakr, & Salama, 2015; Liu, He, Durham, Zhao, & Roberts, 2008; Mansur et al., 2017).

XPS analysis can provide information about the surface area of the powder (Ye, Wang, Xiong, & Sun, 2016). Fig. 1(b-i) shows the XPS full spectra of CMCh and CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+, also the high-resolution fine spectra of C1s, O1s and N1s to further explore the coordination of CMCh and Ln3+. As can be seen in Fig. 1(b), the intensity of Na decreases and the peaks of Eu and Tb appear, so that it can be inferred that Ln3+ replaced Na+ in the samples and loaded into CMCh successfully. In Fig. 1(c), 1276.7 eV and 1242.7 eV can be assigned to Tb 3d3/2 and Tb 3d5/2, and the binding energies of 1165.4 eV and 1135.4 eV belong to Eu 3d3/2 and Eu 3d5/2, respectively (Wang, Fang, Min, Xu, & Li, 2022; Zhang et al., 2022), which are higher than their values for the pristine species, such as Eu 3d3/2 (1155.0 eV) and Eu 3d5/2 (1126.0 eV). These results are further evidence that a strong interaction occurs between Ln3+ and the active sites of CMCh (Zhao, Gu, Liu, Wen, & Ai, 2020). The satellite peaks are located at 1154.8 eV and 1125.1 eV, separately, which appear at the high kinetic energy end of the main peak and originate from the final state effect of the photoelectron emission process. It can be seen that both Eu and Tb exist as the positive trivalent oxidation state in the complex, meaning that the coordination relationship between Eu3+/Tb3+ and CMCh was formed (Lai et al., 2021).

C1s of Fig. 1(d) shows the different chemical bonds of CMCh carbon atoms. The binding energies located at 284.8 eV, 285.51 eV, 286.46 eV and 288.25 eV are respectively vested in C-C(CH), CO, CN, and CO, among which CO and CO are derived from C-OH, C-O-C and -COOH (Luo et al., 2019; Mansur et al., 2017). In the C1s spectra after the formation of the complex as shown in Fig. 1(g), the binding energy of the CC single bond did not change significantly, while it of the other three functional groups increased by 0.09, 0.04 and 0.05 eV respectively, indicating that the functional groups containing oxygen and nitrogen played a major role in the process of coordinating with Ln3+.

As shown in the O1s spectra in Fig. 1(e) and (h), it can be further deconvoluted into three components, and the three peaks can be seen at 531.29 eV, 532.19 eV and 533.05 eV, which belong to C-OH, C-O-C and O-C=O (Fan et al., 2019; Hu, Omer, Ouyang, & Yu, 2018; Huang et al., 2018; Pan et al., 2021). When Eu3+, Tb3+ coordinated with CMCh, the peaks shifted by 0.25 eV, 0.37 eV and 0.13 eV, respectively, due to that the coordination reduced the electron cloud density of O and enhanced the positive electricity of the nucleus. It is also found in the N1s spectra that the binding energy of C-NH2 in Fig. 1(i) is increased by 0.14 eV compared with Fig. 1(f), which indicates that the amino groups in CMCh are involved in metal coordination (Song, Liu, Zhu, & Li, 2019; Zeng et al., 2019; Zhang et al., 2019). Combining with the change of C1s, it can be judged that all of hydroxyl, ether bond, carboxyl and amino groups are involved in the formation of Ln-O by metal coordination (Mondal, Ghorai, & Saha, 2018; Ye et al., 2016).

In order to understand in detail of the coordination environment formed by Eu3+, Tb3+ and CMCh, the Judd-Ofelt (J-O) theory was introduced (Priya & Pandey, 2018; Shi et al., 2018), which is a calculation method widely used in rare earth doped materials. The intensity parameter Ωλ depends on the local geometry of Ln3+, the bonding atom and the polarizability of the first coordination sphere (Bondzior et al., 2022; Kasprzycka et al., 2019). Due to the doping of configuration, Ω4 and Ω6 are less sensitive to crystal field environment, while Ω2 is relatively sensitive, which depends on the asymmetry and covalence of the coordination field of Ln3+. A higher value of Ω2 means the increase of covalent bonds and the high asymmetry of Ln3+ in host material (Shi et al., 2018; Vu Phi et al., 2020). The Ω2 value was calculated using data such as fluorescence excitation spectra, emission spectra and the fluorescence lifetime of the material, and the calculation process is shown in Supplementary Material S3. It can be seen from the results in Table 1 that the complexes CMCh-Eu3+ have higher asymmetry and more coordination covalent bonds. It also confirms the chemical structure deduced characteristics of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ above.

According to IR spectroscopy and XPS characterization and analysis, Ln3+ replaced Na+ in -OH and successfully loaded onto CMCh. In addition, the ether bond, amino groups, and carboxyl groups in CMCh are also involved in coordination, and the carboxyl groups are coordinated in the way of bidentate bridge. In addition, Eu3+ has higher asymmetry and more covalent bonds than Tb3+ according to the J-O theory, resulting the coordination number of Eu3+ is 8, and that of Tb3+ is 6 (Godart et al., 2022; Li, Zhang, et al., 2021). When Eu3+ and Tb3+ are loaded on the surface of CMCh, there is a good compatibility between CMCh and Ln3+, forming a strong interaction with CMCh macromolecules according to Fig. S2. Based on the above conclusion, the chemical structure of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ is inferred as Fig. 2 .

To study the optical properties of the CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+, the excitation spectra of CMCh-Eu3+ and CMCh-Tb3+ are shown as Fig. 3(a). It is found that absorption from ultraviolet to visible region make a contribution to the luminescence of CMCh-Tb3+, but the biggest effect parts are at 239, 258 and 266 nm which is attributed to the absorption of 4f8 → 4f75d1 energy level transition of Tb3+ (Li et al., 2023). The strong excitation band around 225-270 nm is the allowable energy level transition, while the weak around 270-290 nm is the forbidden energy level transition. In addition, the excitation between 290 and 350 nm belongs to the 4f → 4f energy level transition of Tb3+. From the excitation spectra of Eu3+, it can be seen that the excitation peak is mainly concentrated in the range of 225-310 nm and 310-395 nm, of which the excitation peak between 225 and 310 nm belongs to the charge transfer band and absorption between the ion of O2− → Eu3+ (Shruthi, Reddy, Kumar, & Jayasankar, 2020). Because of the energy transfer produced by the 2p orbital electron transition of O2− to 4f empty orbital of Eu3+, it can be easily found that the excitation peak of Tb3+ in this area is stronger than Eu3+. The peak around 310-395 nm (Uv-visible absorption region) vests in the characteristic absorption of 4f → 4f transition of Eu3+, mainly including 7F0 → 5HJ of 331 nm, 7F0 → 5D4 of 363 nm and 7F0 → 5L6 of 390 nm (Lai et al., 2021), of which the peak between 330 and 360 nm is wide. It can be seen from Fig. 3(b). that four characteristic emission peaks of Tb3+ at 496 nm (5D4 → 7F6), 553 nm (5D4 → 7F5), 593 nm (5D4 → 7F4) and 629 nm(5D4 → 7F3), as well as four characteristic emission peaks of Eu3+ at 600 nm(5D0 → 7F1), 624 nm(5D0 → 7F2), 658 nm(5D0 → 7F3), 705 nm(5D0 → 7F4) is clear very much. 330 nm was chosen as the excitation wavelength for this experiment to obtain all the emission spectra after that, for reasons shown in S4.

In our previous study, it was found that the pH value affects the structure of CMCh and has a strong impact on the fluorescence performance of CMCh-Ln3+ (Li, Zhang, et al., 2021). Therefore, the fluorescence emission spectra excited by 330 nm of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ under different pH values are measured as shown in Fig. 3(c). The pH = 7 was determined to be the optimal pH for the experiment to be carried out, and the analysis procedure is described in Supplementary Material S4.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Energy transfer between CMCh and Ln3+
ce:section.ce:para: In order to explore the influence of the addition of Ln3+ on the UV absorption capacity of CMCh, and initially explore the energy transfer process between CMCh and Ln3+, the UV–vis absorption spectra of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ and CMCh at different molar ratio under the solid condition were measured and shown as Fig. 4(a). The absorption of CMCh concentrate mainly upon the range of 200-340 nm, of which the peaks at 204 nm and 212 nm are put down to the n → π* electron transition of CO and the CMCh-OH absorption peak (Zhao et al., 2019). After coordination with Tb3+, the absorption peak at 204 nm still can be observed, and the absorption peak at 212 nm is redshifted to 220 nm, which indicates that the addition of Tb3+ does not influence the UV absorption ability of CMCh. The UV absorption of CMCh-Tb3+ at 211-235 nm is higher than that of CMCh. With the increase of Eu3+ and the decrease of Tb3+, the range of UV absorption is gradually extended to 200-300 nm. The absorption peaks of CMCh-Eu3+ at 203 nm and 219 nm are stronger than those of CMCh and CMCh-Tb3+, and a new absorption peak at 225–228 nm appears. Therefore, CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ has stronger UV absorption capacity than CMCh after coordinating with Ln3+. The dense absorption bands of CMCh and CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ are concentrated in the range of 200-250 nm, indicating that charge transfer occurs between Ln3+ and O2−, which is consistent with the conclusion from infrared spectroscopy, and CMCh plays a key role in the energy transfer process of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ (Ye et al., 2016).

In addition, the CMCh dense absorption band is found to be coincident with the excitation peak of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ in the range of 225-300 nm by excitation spectra and UV spectra Fig. 4(b), which further indicates that CMCh backbone can form a p-π conjugate structure with Eu3+ and Tb3+, while excitation in conjugated polymers can migrate along the polymer chain, so that CMCh can effectively transfer energy to Ln3+ as a ligand (Lai et al., 2021; Li, Ye, Xiong, & Abidi, 2017).

The fluorescence of Ln3+ complexes is generated by the transfer of excitation energy from the excited ligand state to the Ln3+ in the excited state, and the excitation energy of the ligands is transferred to the Ln3+ through the fluorescence resonance between the triplet energy level of the ligands and the Ln3+. In order to analyze ulteriorly the energy transfer between Ln3+ and the ligands by the triplet level, the triplet level of the ligands needs to be known, which needs to be determined with the help of Gd3+. Since Gd3+ has the highest first excited state (32,000 cm−1) level among Ln3+, it means that no energy transfer occurs in most complexes. However, the triplet emission of Gd3+ complexes is usually observed only at very low temperatures, so the phosphorescence spectra of Gd3+ complexes needs to be measured at low temperatures (77 K) to calculate the triplet energy level of ligands (Sun et al., 2020). The preparation method of CMCh-Gd3+ is described in S6. One of the conditions for Ln3+ to receive the energy transferred by the ligand and successfully emit a linear spectrum is that there must be as much overlap between the lowest triplet energy level of the complex and the resonance energy level of the Ln3+ as possible (Gou et al., 2021). Therefore, the possibility of intramolecular energy transfer was initially verified by the overlap of the phosphorescence spectra of CMCh-Gd3+ complexes with CMCh-Eu3+ and CMCh-Tb3+ at liquid nitrogen temperature (Susumu & Masanobu, 1970). As shown in the Fig. 4(c), within the 400-550 nm band, the absorption peaks of the three curves largely overlap, and the maximum shift does not exceed that intramolecular energy transfer is confirmed. It is found that there is a broad emission peak at 483-497 nm from the phosphorescence spectra of CMCh-Gd3+ excited at 330 nm at low temperature, corresponding triplet energy level of CMCh is 20,703 cm−1, which is higher than the excited state of Eu3+ (5D0, 17,300 cm−1) (Li, Wang, Wang, & Li, 2021) and slightly higher than Tb3+ (5D4, 20,400 cm−1) (Chen & Feng, 2015). When organic ligands are coordinated with Ln3+, energy transfer from the ligands to the central Ln3+ will occur because the triplet energy of the ligands is usually higher than (or close to) the complex excited state energy level of Ln3+, resulting in the emission of Ln3+ (Chen & Feng, 2015; Sun et al., 2020). Dexter believed that the energy difference (△E) between the resonance level of Ln3+ and the triplet state of organic ligands should be at least 10k BT (2000 cm−1) to effectively prevent energy from returning (Dexter, 1953). The energy difference between CMCh and Tb3+ is <2000 cm−1, therefore energy will be transferred from Ln3+ to the resonance level of the ligands triplet (energy transfer back). This conclusion not only strongly proves the existence of energy transfer from CMCh to Ln3+ in CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+, but also verifies the assumption that energy from the Ln3+ is transferred back to CMCh to emit blue light which combined with the red light of Eu3+ and green light of Tb3+, so that it has the potential to prepare white light LED.

The main energy of Ln3+ luminescence comes from the intramolecular energy transfer in complexes, and the energy is transferred from the ligands to the inner 4f energy level of Ln3+, so that the central ions emit characteristic fluorescence, which is known as the Antenna effect (Khudyakov, Lobach, & Nadtochenko, 2009). As shown in the Fig. 4(d), when CMCh is coordinated with Ln3+, the fluorescence of the ligands decreases significantly while the fluorescence characteristic peak of Ln3+ appears, which also proves the existence of antenna effect (Yi, Zhang, Chen, Lin, & Chen, 2018). Antenna effect is necessary for the energy transfer from the ligands to Ln3+, and this process may be derived from the fluorescence resonance between the triplet energy levels of the ligands and the excited state levels of Ln3+. Fluorescence resonance energy transfer, also known as Förster resonance energy transfer (FRET), means that in two different fluorescent molecules (groups), if the emission spectra of the donor molecule overlaps the absorption spectra of the acceptor molecule, when the two molecules are close enough, the transfer of fluorescence energy from the donor to the acceptor can be observed (Souza & Sigoli, 2012). To verify whether the model of FRET, the emission spectra of CMCh and the excitation spectra of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ at 553 nm and 624 nm were observed, respectively. As shown in Fig. 4(e), there is clear overlap between the donor emission peak and the acceptor absorption peak, so the presence of FRET was confirmed.

According to the above inferences, based on the analysis of UV and fluorescence spectra, it was concluded that the CMCh undergoes n-π* transition, the addition of Ln3+ enhanced the ability of CMCh to absorb UV light, and Ln-O charge transfer occured. The p-π conjugate structure between CMCh and Ln3+ enables the excitation of UV light to migrate along the polymer chain, thereby transferring energy to Ln3+. The intramolecular energy transfer in CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ was confirmed and the antenna effect occurred between CMCh and Ln3+ through the fluorescence resonance between the triplet energy levels of the ligands and the excited state energy level of Ln3+. After the energy was transferred to Ln3+, Eu3+ normally emitted characteristic fluorescence, while Tb3+ not only emits autofluorescence, but also transmits energy back to CMCh.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Energy transfer between Eu3+ and Tb3+
ce:section.ce:para: In the previous subsection, we analyzed the energy transfer between CMCh and Ln3+, and the content of CMCh was fixed and the molar ratio of Eu3+ and Tb3+ was changed to further explore the energy transfer mechanism between Eu3+ and Tb3+. The fluorescence emission spectra of the samples at 330 nm are shown in Fig. 5(a) and (b). When Eu3+: Tb3+ = 9: 1, the fluorescence intensity of characteristic peak of Eu3+ is higher than that of Eu3+ coordination so that it is speculated that the energy of Tb3+ can be transferred to Eu3+ when CMCh is used as ligands and to coordinate with Eu3+/Tb3+. To test this hypothesis preliminarily, the Eu3+ content was fixed and the Tb3+ content was varied (Eu3+: Tb3+ = 5: 1, 5: 2, 5: 3, 5: 4, 5: 5, 5: 7 and 5: 9), then fluorescence spectra at 330 nm are shown in Fig. 5(c) and (d). Obviously, the fluorescence characteristic peak intensity of Eu3+ at 623.5 nm increased first and then decreased with the increase of Tb3+, indicating that a small number of Tb3+ can sensitize Eu3+. However, Tb3+ preempts coordination groups with Eu3+ with the content of Tb3+ increasing, resulting in less energy transfer from ligands to Eu3+, so that the fluorescence intensity of Eu3+ decreases.

In order to further confirm the existence of Tb3+ sensitized Eu3+ in CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+, CMCh-Tb3+ and CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ (5: 5) is selected under 330 nm excitation to record its luminous decay curve at 553 nm, as shown in Fig. 5(e) and (f). The fluorescence lifetime of CMCh-Tb3+ at 533 nm is 1.1579 ms, while that of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ is 0.5390 ms, shortened 0.6189 ms, calculated from S5, which indicates that the lifetime of Tb3+ is reduced significantly due to the presence of Eu3+. The fluorescence lifetime of CMCh-Eu3+ is 0.5046 ms, which is increased by 0.0344 ms after coordinating with Tb3+ (Li, Zhang, et al., 2021). These phenomena can be attributed to the energy transfer from Tb3+ to Eu3+ via a non-radiative transition, resulting in the energy loss of Tb3+ (Lai et al., 2021). It is proved strongly that the energy transfer process from Tb3+ to Eu3+ exists at 330 nm with CMCh as the ligands.

According to the above analysis, the basic principle of luminescence of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ was speculated. The ligands first undergo a n-π* transition to absorb UV light from the singlet ground state S0 to the excited state S1, and the excited energy is transferred to T1 by means of intersystem crossing (ISC; Non-radiative transition process). Part of the energy of the triplet excited state returns to the ground state to release the phosphorescence of the ligands, the other part of the energy is transferred to Eu3+ and Tb3+ through the fluorescence resonance between the triplet energy level of the ligands and the excited state energy level of Ln3+, where the Eu3+ undergoes the 4f → 4f radiation transition and emits characteristic fluorescence. While in addition to the 5D4 → 7FJ (J = 1,2,3,4) radiation transition, Tb3+ also transferred a small amount of energy to Eu3+ and back to CMCh owing to the triplet energy range is not large enough. The fluorescence transfer mechanism is represented as Jablonski energy level diagram as shown in Fig. 6 .

ce:section.ce:section-title: Application of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ in multicolor LED
ce:section.ce:para: Due to the existence of energy transfer back phenomenon, the sample CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ can emit different colors of light under different conditions, so it is possible to prepare multi-color LED lights. The multicolor LED lamps which are prepared from CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ (1: 0; 3: 7; 0: 1) and CMCh were showed as Fig. 7(a). LED lamp of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ (1: 0; 3: 7; 0: 1) emits red light, approximate white light, and green light separately, whereas that of CMCh glows blue light.

Combined with the CIE spectra shown in Fig. 7(b) and the relevant parameters in Table 2 , it was found that the color of LED light changed from red to yellow (approximate white) to green and blue bluish light in the end by adjusting the molar ratios of Eu3+ and Tb3+ (Eu: Tb = 1: 0, 9: 1, 7: 3, 5: 5, 3: 7, 1: 9, 0: 1), that CIE coordinates changes from (0.53, 0.36) to (0.33, 0.51). With the increase of Tb3+ content, the related color temperature of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ increased continuously, but the maximum color temperature was not more than 6500 K. The color rendering index (CRI) first increased and then decreased, of which the highest chromogenic index is 88.6 when Eu3+: Tb3+ = 3: 7. High-quality WLED requires that the light source must conform to the Commission International Eclairage (CIE) coordinates (0.33,0.33), CRI >80 and related color temperature (CCT =2500–6500 K) (Wu et al., 2022) according to literature (Othong, Boonmak, Promarak, Kielar, & Youngme, 2019) so that CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ system has a broad application prospect in the preparation of multicolor LED lamps. Fig. 7(c) shows the LED spectra of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ cured by epoxy resin to 365 nm purple light chip after electrification, and the light intensity variation diagram at 544 and 615 nm is shown as Fig. 7(d), so that it was found that the circumstance of energy transfer from Tb3+ to Eu3+ still exists. At the same time, the ligands peak at 450 nm disappears, indicating that CMCh is a favorable ligand for Eu3+ and Tb3+.

CMCh not only has the excellent properties of natural polymers, but also can transfer energy to Ln3+ well as biological macromolecule ligands. It was used to coordinate Eu3+ and Tb3+, and a lanthanide complex CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ was prepared. Combined with representation and J-O theory, the CMCh backbone can form a p-π conjugate structure with Eu3+ and Tb3+. The ether bond, amino groups and carboxyl groups in CMCh are also involved in coordination, and the carboxyl groups are coordinated in the way of bidentate bridge. In addition, Eu3+ has higher asymmetry and more covalent bonds than Tb3+, so that the chemical structure of CMCh-Eu3+/Tb3+ was inferred.

It is proved that the CMCh backbone can form conjugate structure with Ln3+, and the energy transfer occurs between Ln3+ and CMCh. The triplet level energy levels calculation of the phosphorescent spectra and the fluorescence excitation and emission spectra of the sample not only verify the hypothesis of energy transfer from Tb3+ back to CMCh, but also demonstrate the existence of antenna effect. The energy is transferred from CMCh to Ln3+ through the fluorescence resonance between the triplet energy level of the ligands and the excited state energy level of Ln3+. The fluorescence spectra of different molar ratio of Eu3+/Tb3+ and the results of fluorescence lifetime display that a spot of Tb3+ does sensitize to Eu3+. In addition, the phenomenon of energy transfer from Tb3+ to Eu3+ was confirmed combining with LED spectra. A color adjustable LED lamp was obtained, and CIE chromaticity diagram indicates that the maximum CRI is 88.6, which broadened the application range of biological macromolecules as ligands.
